title: Reflogs, Reachability Windows, and Recovery · Changed-History Lab
summary: An independent maintainer receives a different graph, repository policy, and failure constraint and must transfer reflogs, reachability windows, and recovery into a new reflog-led recovery timeline and retained ref. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of reflog-led recovery timeline and retained ref with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain per-ref and HEAD reflogs, selector syntax, creation policy, worktree scope, expiration categories, and configuration.
Correlate ref updates, checkout history, operation messages, timestamps, actors, and graph evidence without treating reflog order as commit ancestry.
Discover lost tips with reflog, fsck, object inspection, and known content while avoiding commands that may expire or prune candidates.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
lab case — Case G86 is a lab in a disposable repository. the lost topic tip remains named by a local reflog entry inside the expiration window. The learner must predict the timeline reveals the prior topic ID and force-update operation independently of ancestry, run git reflog show --date=iso --all, explain topic@{1} names b00b1e5 before the forced update and its tree matches the missing feature, test this changed condition: expire the topic name in the scenario and recover the candidate through fsck content evidence, and preserve this recovery boundary: create refs/recovery/lost-topic at b00b1e5 before inspecting or integrating it.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for explain per-ref and head reflogs, selector syntax, creation policy, worktree scope, expiration categories, and configuration.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-expiration-retention-policy-r4l4dy" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Expiration Retention Policy into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-expiration-retention-policy-r4l4dy
# scenario-key: trace-r4l4dy probe-yd4l4r boundary-l4dyr4 owner-r4l4dyx fault-r4l4dyz
# before-ref: main=a11cer4 candidate=b00b1dy
# hypothesis: the lost topic tip remains named by a local reflog entry inside the expiration window
git reflog show --date=iso --all
# expected: the timeline reveals the prior topic ID and force-update operation independently of ancestry
# observation: topic@{1} names b00b1e5 before the forced update and its tree matches the missing feature
# changed-case: expire the topic name in the scenario and recover the candidate through fsck content evidence
# after-ref: main=a11cer4 candidate=c0ffeey
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: create refs/recovery/lost-topic at b00b1e5 before inspecting or integrating it
# transfer-limit: reflogs are local retention aids, not shared audit logs or backup guarantees
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

steps.title: Test an acceptance boundary
steps.instruction:
lab case — Case G94 is a lab in a disposable repository. the lost topic tip remains named by a local reflog entry inside the expiration window. The learner must predict the timeline reveals the prior topic ID and force-update operation independently of ancestry, run git reflog show --date=iso --all, explain topic@{1} names b00b1e5 before the forced update and its tree matches the missing feature, test this changed condition: expire the topic name in the scenario and recover the candidate through fsck content evidence, and preserve this recovery boundary: create refs/recovery/lost-topic at b00b1e5 before inspecting or integrating it. Competency probe: Design reflog expiration, gc grace, backup, and incident policy from collaboration and compliance needs without promising archival durability.

One precondition fails while output still looks plausible. What is the correct response?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for design reflog expiration, gc grace, backup, and incident policy from collaboration and compliance needs without promising archival durability.

Competency probe: Design reflog expiration, gc grace, backup, and incident policy from collaboration and compliance needs without promising archival durability.

content.title: Fault-isolation decision
content.text: Select evidence that identifies a cause, not a patch that only hides the Gita Expiration Retention Policy symptom in reflog-led recovery timeline and retained ref during this lab case.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: git-rerere Manual — Controls recorded-resolution preimages, postimages, reuse, forgetting, autoupdate, and retention.

hints: Name the Gita Expiration Retention Policy precondition or invariant that the lab case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Case G94 is a lab in a disposable repository. the lost topic tip remains named by a local reflog entry inside the expiration window. The learner must predict the timeline reveals the prior topic ID and force-update operation independently of ancestry, run git reflog show --date=iso --all, explain topic@{1} names b00b1e5 before the forced update and its tree matches the missing feature, test this changed condition: expire the topic name in the scenario and recover the candidate through fsck content evidence, and preserve this recovery boundary: create refs/recovery/lost-topic at b00b1e5 before inspecting or integrating it. Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Increasing reflog expiry converts local Git storage into a verified backup.

stimulus.title: Gita Expiration Retention Policy failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Increasing reflog expiry converts local Git storage into a verified backup.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design reflog expiration, gc grace, backup, and incident policy from collaboration and compliance needs without promising archival durability.

options.text: For test an acceptance boundary, apply Gita Expiration Retention Policy without its stated preconditions, omit the reflog-led recovery timeline and retained ref trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Gita Expiration Retention Policy for reflog-led recovery timeline and retained ref.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Gita Expiration Retention Policy condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

Keep the plausible Gita Expiration Retention Policy output and hide the violated reflog-led recovery timeline and retained ref assumption instead of explaining the failure. This does not resolve test an acceptance boundary.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write the lab evidence note for Gita Expiration Retention Policy: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Case G95 is a lab in a disposable repository. the lost topic tip remains named by a local reflog entry inside the expiration window. The learner must predict the timeline reveals the prior topic ID and force-update operation independently of ancestry, run git reflog show --date=iso --all, explain topic@{1} names b00b1e5 before the forced update and its tree matches the missing feature, test this changed condition: expire the topic name in the scenario and recover the candidate through fsck content evidence, and preserve this recovery boundary: create refs/recovery/lost-topic at b00b1e5 before inspecting or integrating it. Competency probe: Design reflog expiration, gc grace, backup, and incident policy from collaboration and compliance needs without promising archival durability.
steps.why: Retrieving Gita Expiration Retention Policy through the concrete reflog-led recovery timeline and retained ref case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Design reflog expiration, gc grace, backup, and incident policy from collaboration and compliance needs without promising archival durability.
